configuration"]},"complexity_notes":"This is architecture documentation rather than an executable source file. The embedded CRDT applyOperation pseudocode has mixed complexity: idempotency lookup is typically O(1), insert uses binary search O(log n) followed by array splices O(n), delete and format are O(k) plus position lookup cost, and move requires removal/reinsertion work proportional to affected and shifted elements. The design also identifies operational complexity around causal synchronization, shard migration, Pulsar geo-replication, PostgreSQL single-writer routing, snapshots, and multi-region recovery. There is a terminology/design tension: the document describes LSEQ identifiers 'without tombstones' but the delete implementation explicitly retains tombstones."}
